refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (link availability) and ties it explicitly to the customer-defined target and operational timeframe, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period using telemetry logs and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or switch to backup link to maintain availability.
FR4: Provide real-time link availability status to operators and system managers.

candidate_dps: Redundant communication channels with automatic failover
Real-time link health monitoring and alerting system
Adaptive bandwidth management to prioritize teleoperation traffic
Robust error detection and correction protocols
selected_dp.selected_dp: Redundant communication channels with automatic failover

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates link availability management from other system functions, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of multiple communication channels at the worksite.
Risk of increased system complexity leading to integration challenges.
Risk that failover mechanisms may introduce latency affecting teleoperation.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating real-time monitoring DP in next refinement level to enhance detection and response.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing teleoperation communication links, including redundancy and failover mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical communication channels used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send link status and availability metrics to monitoring and logging systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands that depend on link availability.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of link availability logic and low diffusion; well encapsulated.
Contains some link management but more diffuse due to broader scope.
Limited containment; mostly consumers of link status.
Minimal containment; primarily consumers of safety alerts.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability management in the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential single point of failure if Teleoperation module is compromised.
Integration complexity with Network Link interface may affect containment.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate failover integration with Safety Services for safety-critical link loss scenarios.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundant communication channels with failover) directly addresses the refined FR sub-functions and meets the availability target.

diffusion_risks: Failover logic diffusion if implemented partially outside Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment scores and appropriate interface usage.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Link availability can be verified via telemetry data and operational testing of failover mechanisms within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of link availability responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Consider adding explicit monitoring DP and IM in next refinement level.
Review integration points with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, adding explicit monitoring and alerting functions as separate FRs and DPs will improve clarity and verification. Also, integration with Safety Services for safety-critical link loss needs explicit consideration.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or switch to backup link to maintain availability.
FR4: Provide real-time link availability status to operators and system managers.

revise.why: To explicitly capture monitoring and alerting functions as separate FRs for better traceability and verification.

assumptions: Multiple communication channels are available for redundancy.
Telemetry interface can support real-time link status reporting.
open_risks: Failover latency impact on teleoperation control.
Integration complexity with Safety Services for safety alerts.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data demonstrating link availability over evaluation period.
Test results of failover mechanisms under realistic conditions.
